_comment: Data-driven catalog of API-key AI providers for bento-auth — paste a key, it is validated, stored in state.providers, and propagated to every BENTO_MANAGED stack. Subscriptions (OAuth) are NOT handled here; each app owns its native sign-in. ADDING A PROVIDER = ONE OBJECT BELOW (no code change). Most providers get their OWN dedicated env var(s) so they coexist — registering one never evicts another. Fields: id (subcommand), label, format ('native' = the app recognises a dedicated key env var built-in, no base URL needed; 'compat' = OpenAI-compatible custom endpoint, gets a dedicated key env PLUS a dedicated base-url env — wire it as a custom provider in your app pointing apiKey/baseUrl at those env vars), env (key var), base_url / base_url_env (compat only — both dedicated, never the shared OPENAI_* slot), signup_url, validate_url (GET with Bearer to verify; empty = skip), note. The shared OPENAI_API_KEY + OPENAI_BASE_URL slot belongs to the native 'openai' entry and the ad-hoc 'bento-auth openai-compat <label> <base_url>' command; compat entries never touch it.

providers.label: Anthropic (Claude API key)
providers.note: Classic console API key (sk-ant-api...), pay-as-you-go. NOT the Claude Pro/Max subscription — for that use the app's native sign-in (e.g. paperclip's claude_local). Validation skipped (Anthropic uses x-api-key, not Bearer).

providers.label: OpenAI (Platform API key)
providers.note: Classic platform API key (sk-...), pay-as-you-go on api.openai.com. NOT the ChatGPT Plus subscription — for that use the app's native OAuth (e.g. openclaw 'models auth login --provider openai'). Occupies the shared OpenAI slot.

providers.label: OpenRouter
providers.note: Universal aggregator — one key, hundreds of models. Built-in provider.

providers.label: OpenCode (Zen + Go)
providers.note: One key unlocks BOTH the Zen catalog and the Go subscription; pick models with the opencode/ (Zen) or opencode-go/ (Go) prefix.

providers.label: Google Gemini (API key)
providers.note: Direct API key (Google uses ?key=, not Bearer, so validation is skipped). For the subscription/OAuth tier use the opencode broker (planned).

providers.label: DeepSeek
providers.note: Cheap, strong at coding. Built-in provider.

providers.label: z.ai GLM Coding Plan
providers.note: Cheap GLM coding SUBSCRIPTION (~USD 10/mo). base_url is the CODING-PLAN endpoint (/api/coding/paas/v4) — the subscription bills here. The pay-as-you-go API platform is /api/paas/v4 (separate prepaid balance); override ZAI_BASE_URL to that only if you use credits instead of the plan. Lives in its OWN slot (ZAI_API_KEY + ZAI_BASE_URL); wire it as a custom OpenAI-compatible provider in your app. Verified: glm-5.1 replies via the coding endpoint; paas/v4 returns 'insufficient balance' on a plan-only account.

providers.label: Kimi (Moonshot)
providers.note: Own slot (KIMI_API_KEY + KIMI_BASE_URL) — wire as a custom provider.
